name: qonto
description: Read Qonto business banking data: organization bank accounts with balances, transactions, and transaction attachments

envVars.name: QONTO_API_KEY
envVars.description: Qonto API credential in the form organization-slug:secret-key — your organization slug (login) and API secret key joined by a colon. Sent as the raw Authorization header value (no Bearer prefix, no Base64 encoding).

tools.name: Get Organization
tools.description: Retrieve the authenticated organization with its bank accounts, including IBAN, BIC, currency, status, and current/authorized balances

include_external_accounts.description: Set to true to also include connected external accounts

tools.name: List Transactions
tools.description: List transactions for one bank account (identified by bank_account_id or IBAN) with status, side, and date filtering

bank_account_id.description: UUID of the bank account (from Get Organization). Provide this or iban; bank_account_id takes precedence if both are set

iban.description: IBAN of the bank account, as an alternative to bank_account_id

status[].description: Filter by status: pending, declined, completed (default: completed)

side.description: Filter by direction: credit or debit

settled_at_from.description: Only transactions settled at or after this ISO 8601 timestamp

settled_at_to.description: Only transactions settled at or before this ISO 8601 timestamp

emitted_at_from.description: Only transactions emitted at or after this ISO 8601 timestamp

emitted_at_to.description: Only transactions emitted at or before this ISO 8601 timestamp

with_attachments.description: Filter by attachment presence: true for transactions with receipts, false for those without

includes[].description: Embed associated resources: vat_details, labels, attachments

sort_by.description: Sort as property:order, where property is created_at, updated_at, settled_at, or emitted_at and order is asc or desc

historicalSummary.omitted: VAT details, counterparty enrichment, and attachment metadata

tools.name: Get Transaction
tools.description: Retrieve a single transaction by its UUID, optionally embedding VAT details, labels, or attachments

includes[].description: Embed associated resources: vat_details, labels, attachments

tools.name: List Transaction Attachments
tools.description: List the attachments (receipts, invoices) of a transaction with download URLs

tools.name: Upload Transaction Attachment
tools.description: Upload a receipt or invoice (JPEG, PNG, or PDF) onto a transaction to justify it for bookkeeping; the file is processed in the background, so the attachment is not visible immediately

transactionId.description: Transaction UUID to attach the file to

file.description: Base64-encoded file content (JPEG, PNG, or PDF), sent decoded as the binary multipart 'file' part

file_name.description: Filename for the uploaded attachment, e.g. receipt.pdf

tools.name: Get Attachment
tools.description: Retrieve an attachment by id to get a fresh download URL — URLs returned by the attachment endpoints expire after 30 minutes, so re-fetch before any delayed download

attachmentId.description: Attachment UUID from a transaction's attachment_ids or List Transaction Attachments

tools.name: List Supplier Invoices
tools.description: List the organization's supplier invoices (accounts payable inbox) with status and payment matching; available on all price plans except Solo Basic

filter[status].description: Status to filter on: to_review, to_approve, awaiting_payment, pending, scheduled, paid, or archived

filter[created_at_from].description: Only invoices created at or after this time (RFC 3339, e.g. 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z)

filter[created_at_to].description: Only invoices created at or before this time (RFC 3339)

sort_by.description: Sort property and direction, e.g. created_at:desc

per_page.description: Number of supplier invoices per page

prompts.title: Account balances
prompts.prompt: Show my Qonto bank accounts with their current and authorized balances.

prompts.title: Recent transactions
prompts.prompt: List the most recent completed transactions on my main Qonto account and summarize debits vs credits.

prompts.title: Missing receipts
prompts.prompt: Find recent Qonto transactions that have no attachment so I can chase missing receipts.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Qonto setup

steps.title: Open your Qonto organization
steps.description: Log in to the Qonto web app at https://app.qonto.com. For testing without a live account, create a sandbox organization through the Qonto Developer Portal toolkit at https://developers.qonto.com.

steps.title: Generate an API key
steps.description: In the web app, go to Integrations and Partnerships > API key and click Generate. Note both your organization slug (the login, e.g. my-company-1234) and the generated secret key.

steps.title: Set the environment variable
steps.description: Set QONTO_API_KEY to the organization slug and secret key joined by a colon, e.g. my-company-1234:62885d39f3a0ddedd3d3ee0432a43ccr. The value is sent as-is in the Authorization header — no Bearer prefix and no Base64 encoding.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run Get Organization. A successful response confirms the credential; a 401 means the Authorization value is malformed.

notes: The Authorization header is the raw 'organization-slug:secret-key' string — unlike most APIs there is no Bearer prefix and no encoding.
This connector targets the production API (thirdparty.qonto.com/v2). The sandbox uses a different host (thirdparty-sandbox.staging.qonto.co) and additionally requires an X-Qonto-Staging-Token header.

Uploading an attachment to a transaction (POST /v2/transactions/{id}/attachments) requires a multipart/form-data file part, which this connector's JSON endpoint executor does not support yet — only listing attachments is included.
